args.query:
SELECT 
    "Index",
    SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as up_days,
    SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as down_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('GSPTSE', 'IXIC', 'NYA')
  AND "Date" LIKE '%2018%'
GROUP BY "Index"

exec_args.sql:
SELECT 
    "Index",
    SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as up_days,
    SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as down_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('GSPTSE', 'IXIC', 'NYA')
  AND "Date" LIKE '%2018%'
GROUP BY "Index"

result.preview: "Among the North American stock indices, only the NASDAQ Composite (IXIC) had more up days than down days in 2018, with 131 up days compared to 120 down days. The other North American indices, the NYSE Composite (NYA) and the S&P/TSX Composite (GSPTSE), had more down days than up days."

args.answer: Among the North American stock indices, only the NASDAQ Composite (IXIC) had more up days than down days in 2018, with 131 up days compared to 120 down days. The other North American indices, the NYSE Composite (NYA) and the S&P/TSX Composite (GSPTSE), had more down days than up days.

val_args.answer: Among the North American stock indices, only the NASDAQ Composite (IXIC) had more up days than down days in 2018, with 131 up days compared to 120 down days. The other North American indices, the NYSE Composite (NYA) and the S&P/TSX Composite (GSPTSE), had more down days than up days.
